name: Caglar Law Firm PC
description_short: Law firm specializing in immigration, business formation, and corporate law with services including business startup, contracts, and organizational compliance.
description_long: Caglar Law Firm P.C. is a legal services provider based in New York (646-930-2077) that offers comprehensive legal representation across multiple practice areas. The firm was established to serve clients navigating complex legal matters in immigration, business, and corporate domains. While the firm's website emphasizes immigration law as a primary focus area, it maintains significant business law capabilities that initially led to its categorization under business-loans. However, examination of the actual service offerings reveals the firm operates as a general practice law firm rather than a financial services or lending entity. The firm's business law services are specifically focused on legal formation, structuring, and compliance—not financing or lending of any kind. Caglar Law Firm provides guidance on business entity selection (corporations, LLCs, partnerships, non-profits), handles incorporation paperwork, advises on tax implications, and assists with business transactions. The firm also handles contract drafting and review, intellectual property matters, real estate law, commercial litigation, and non-profit governance. What distinguishes Caglar Law Firm is its integrated approach to business legal needs, offering end-to-end support from business formation through ongoing compliance and acquisition assistance. The firm explicitly handles buy-sell agreements, merger and acquisition structuring, business licenses and permits, and organizational documentation. However, an honest assessment reveals several limitations: the website provides minimal detail about attorney credentials, experience levels, or case results; pricing information is entirely absent; and there is no indication of specialization depth in any particular practice area. The firm's web presence, while functional, lacks substantive content about the attorneys themselves or client testimonials. Most critically, this firm is a law practice and should not be categorized among consumer finance products—it provides legal services, not financial products or lending solutions.
pros: Comprehensive business formation services covering multiple entity types (S-Corps, LLCs, partnerships, non-profits, LPs)
End-to-end support from business startup through ongoing compliance and corporate formalities
Handles complex business transactions including mergers, acquisitions, and buy-sell agreements
Advises on tax implications and business tax strategies as part of entity selection process
Assistance with federal, state, and local business licensing and permit compliance
Covers intellectual property protection (trademarks, service marks, patents, copyrights)
Provides contract drafting and negotiation services for employment, service, and business agreements
cons: Website contains no attorney bios, credentials, experience levels, or areas of specialization depth
No pricing information or fee structure provided; no transparency on service costs
Minimal case results, client testimonials, or track record information available
Categorized as 'business-loans' despite being a law firm with zero lending or financing products
Limited detail on turnaround times, availability, or specific service scope boundaries
best_for: Entrepreneurs and small business owners forming new entities and seeking legal structure guidance
Business owners engaged in acquisitions, mergers, or buy-sell transactions requiring legal documentation
Non-profit organizations requiring incorporation and ongoing governance compliance
Small businesses needing business license, permit, and regulatory compliance assistance
diagnosis: Caglar Law Firm is a law practice providing business legal services, not a financial product or lending company. This firm is fundamentally miscategorized and should not appear in a consumer finance database. It is best for business owners and entrepreneurs needing legal formation, structuring, and compliance support—not financial services consumers seeking loans, credit repair, or other finance products.
services: Business entity formation (corporations, S-corps, LLCs, partnerships, non-profits)
Business startup consulting and entity selection advising
Incorporation and organizational document preparation (articles, bylaws, operating agreements)
Business acquisition and sale transaction structuring
Contract drafting and review (asset purchase, operating, employment, service agreements)
Business licensing and permit acquisition and renewal
Intellectual property services (trademark, patent, copyright, service mark registration)
Ongoing corporate compliance and formality guidance
